_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Effective for treating cerebrovascular ischemia due to long-segment or proximal carotid narrowing.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
Angiogram / CT Angiogram / Doppler ultrasound /Magnetic resonance angiography (MRA) reports investigations confirming the diagnosis

claim.label: Invoice/barcode of graft used (if artificial graft used)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
Was the Angiogram / CT Angiogram / Doppler ultrasound/ Magnetic resonance angiography (MRA) reports suggestive of Peripheral arterial diseases

cpd_claim.q: Are Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) submitted?
Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Is the Invoice/barcode of graft used submitted? (if artificial graft used)
Is Detailed Discharge Summary with date of follow-up submitted?

common_queries: Diagnostic imaging reports (Angiogram / CT Angiogram / Doppler ultrasound / MRA) not indicative of Peripheral arterial disease or the specific procedure.
Clinical notes missing or incomplete (history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication, planned management, or admission advice).
Invoice/barcode of artificial graft not submitted when used.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) not submitted.

clinical_key_pointers: Peripheral artery disease (PAD) occurs when plaque accumulates in the arterial system and obstructs blood flow.

Atherosclerosis plaques accumulates inside the arterial intima and obstructs the lumen of the vessel causing a reduction in blood flow, which leads to diminished oxygen supply to the recipient tissues.
An arterial aneurysm is a bulge in the artery that develops in areas where the arterial wall is weak.
Also, certain natural branch points and curvatures within the vascular tree are more susceptible to atherosclerosis due to turbulent blood flow and shear stress.
Aorto-carotid bypass is effective for treating cerebrovascular ischemia due to long-segment or proximal carotid narrowing.
The morbidity of carotid bypasses: stroke (0.5-8%) and nerve injury (up to 10%).
surgical_options: Carotid bypasses have morbidity risks including stroke (0.5-8%) and nerve injury (up to 10%).
